intro_script: Welcome to your daily dose of robotics news for July 1, 2026. Today, we're covering everything from soft robotic hardware integration and major ROS advancements, to developer speed tips. Grab your coffee; it’s time for the update!
outro_script: That’s all for your July 1st deep dive into the bot world! Remember those soft robots? The future is truly yielding. Thanks for tuning in, and keep building amazing things! Bye for now.

items.title: Soft, robotic cells from morph embed physical AI into hardware
items.summary: Morph has unveiled a novel, physically intelligent soft robotics platform. This technology allows for the design and manufacture of components called "soft robotic cells." These cells are intended to embed artificial intelligence directly into physical hardware. The initial announcement regarding these advanced cells was published by The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Soft, robotic cells from morph embed physical AI into hardware." This development details a new platform enabling the integration of artificial intelligence directly within physical robotic components. Morph has unveiled its novel soft robotics technology, allowing for the design and manufacture of units termed "soft robotic cells." These specialized cells represent an advancement in physically intelligent systems, indicating a method to build computation directly into the hardware structure itself rather than relying solely on external processing units. The initial announcement confirms this capability as a significant step toward more embodied artificial intelligence. This development suggests a future trajectory for robotics focusing on highly integrated and physically adaptive machine intelligence.

items.title: ROS Industrial: ros2_canopen: Natively Integrating CANopen Devices into the ROS 2 Ecosystem
items.summary: The ros2_canopen stack natively integrates CANopen devices into ROS 2 by utilizing lely-core for low-level protocol handling. It allows developers to configure a CAN bus through a single YAML description, launching the master and device drivers accordingly. Users can operate in standard nodes, lifecycle nodes, or integrated with ros2_control for control tasks. The stack offers two drivers: a generic Proxy Driver and a specialized CiA 402 driver for motion control. Recent updates enhance multi-drive coordination, enabling single nodes to represent multiple axes as individual joints controllable through familiar ROS 2 controllers.
items.audio_script: From Planet ROS, "ROS Industrial: ros2_canopen: Natively Integrating CANopen Devices into the ROS 2 Ecosystem." This report details a method allowing seamless incorporation of CANopen devices directly within the ROS 2 framework. The integration is achieved through the utilization of lely-core for handling low-level protocol requirements, enabling users to define an entire CAN bus setup via one YAML description that subsequently launches both master and device drivers. Developers can execute operations using standard nodes or lifecycle nodes, alongside full compatibility with ros2_control for advanced control implementations, featuring a generic Proxy Driver and a dedicated CiA 402 motion control driver, notably supporting multi-drive coordination within single controlling nodes. This advancement streamlines hardware integration, providing clearer pathways for robotics systems utilizing industry-standard industrial communication protocols.

items.title: How Jaiveer Singh Is Helping Robots — and Developers — Move Faster
items.summary: Jaiveer Singh focuses on foundational aspects of robotics rather than just impressive demonstrations. His expertise lies in the necessary infrastructure, including the internal circuit boards and supporting software. He develops tools that allow developers to process data from robot cameras. Moreover, he tackles the complex engineering required for robots to move beyond controlled test environments. Essentially, his work accelerates the practical deployment capabilities of robotics technology.
items.audio_script: From NVIDIA Blog, "How Jaiveer Singh Is Helping Robots — and Developers — Move Faster." This report details foundational work advancing robotics through essential infrastructure development rather than showcasing only final demonstrations. The focus involves developing core components like internal circuit boards and supporting software necessary for advanced robotic function. Specifically, the described efforts include creating tools that facilitate developers' processing of data streams originating from robot cameras, alongside addressing complex engineering required for operation outside controlled test settings. This body of work directly expedites the practical deployment potential across the robotics technology sector. The advancements point toward a measurable acceleration in the real-world applicability and widespread integration of sophisticated robotic systems.

items.title: X Square Robot brings its valuation to $2.8B with four consecutive funding rounds
items.summary: X Square Robotics utilizes a comprehensive system integrating foundation models, specialized robotics hardware, and a robust data pipeline. This technology is designed for successful real-world deployments across various applications. Following four consecutive funding rounds, the company's valuation has reached $2.8 billion. Information regarding this achievement was initially reported by The Robot Report.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "X Square Robot brings its valuation to $2.8B with four consecutive funding rounds." This report details the recent financial achievements and technological standing of X Square Robotics. The company operates a comprehensive system that merges foundation models, specialized robotics hardware, and a robust data pipeline, all components engineered for successful real-world deployment across diverse applications. Following this sequence of four consecutive financing rounds, X Square Robotics has officially reached an enterprise valuation of $2.8 billion. This substantial funding achievement underscores growing investor confidence in the integration of advanced AI frameworks with physical robotic systems within the industrial sector.

items.title: Queue raises funding to build fully autonomous pharmacy
items.summary: Queue is developing automated pharmacy units designed to improve prescription accessibility. These advanced systems aim to operate autonomously across various locations. Deployment sites will include hospitals, retail stores, and medically underserved communities. Recent reporting confirms that Queue has successfully raised necessary funding for this expansion. The development focuses on bringing routine medication access to a wider public reach.
items.audio_script: From The Robot Report, "Queue raises funding to build fully autonomous pharmacy." This story details the advancement of automated pharmacy units designed to enhance prescription accessibility across diverse community settings. These advanced systems are engineered for complete autonomy in operation. Deployment plans encompass a variety of locations including hospital facilities, retail store environments, and medically underserved regions. Recent reports confirm that the company has successfully secured the requisite funding necessary to move forward with this significant expansion. The core development objective remains increasing public reach for routine medication access through automated means. This represents a notable step in applying autonomous robotics toward improving localized healthcare infrastructure.

items.title: MIT student teams win top honors in NASA competition
items.summary: Three MIT teams achieved significant success at the 2026 NASA RASC-AL Competition. They earned a total of five top awards during the event. The competition focused on developing essential components for lunar bases. Furthermore, the teams addressed designs necessary for upcoming missions to Mars. Their achievements highlight student ingenuity in aerospace engineering.
items.audio_script: From MIT News - Robotics, "MIT student teams win top honors in NASA competition." The report details three MIT teams achieving significant success at the 2026 NASA RASC-AL Competition, earning a total of five top awards during the event. The competition's focus involved developing essential components designated for future lunar bases, while the participating groups also addressed complex designs requisite for forthcoming missions targeted toward Mars. These achievements underscore the depth of student ingenuity within the field of aerospace engineering. The demonstrated capabilities suggest accelerated progress in developing autonomous systems crucial for deep-space exploration initiatives across various technological sectors.

items.title: How to build a WordPress website for an industrial business
items.summary: Industrial businesses recognize the critical role of a website in their operations. A single online platform can effectively manage multiple key business functions. These essential functions include capturing potential leads and attracting new customers. Furthermore, the site should serve to showcase the company's industry expertise. Finally, it can be used to organize and streamline customer service activities.
items.audio_script: From Robotics & Automation News, "How to build a WordPress website for an industrial business." The report details how a single online platform can manage several critical functions for businesses operating in the industrial sector. These essential functionalities include establishing mechanisms for capturing potential leads and generating new customer interest. Furthermore, such a site must effectively showcase the company's established industry expertise and provide a centralized system for organizing customer service activities. This focus underscores the necessity of robust digital infrastructure within traditional industrial operations moving forward.

items.title: Locker-based Truck-Drone Routing with Integrated Considerations of Pickups, Deliveries, and No-Fly Zones
items.summary: Truck-drone delivery leverages smart lockers as automated hubs for drone docking, parcel exchange, and battery swaps, enhancing service range. Addressing real-world complexity, this research proposes a locker-based truck-drone routing problem integrating pickups, deliveries, and no-fly zones (LTDRP-PDNF). The goal is to minimize operational cost for the entire fleet using integrated routing. The method models route construction as a Markov Decision Process solved by a two-stage deep reinforcement learning heuristic. This system first solves the truck-only routing via an attention encoder/BiGRU decoder and then combines this with a hybrid heuristic for final, coordinated truck and drone paths.

items.title: Position: Vision-Language-Action Models Cannot Be Verified to Perform Physical Reasoning
items.summary: Vision-Language-Action (VLA) systems excel on robot manipulation tasks, leading to interpretations that suggest semantic understanding translates directly to physical capability. However, this paper argues that such generalization is unverified because current evaluation metrics cannot distinguish between semantic mapping and actual physical decision-making. Task success rates are insufficient because improvements could stem from various sources like distributional overlap or mere pattern matching. The authors propose novel evaluation designs that introduce controlled variation to causally separate and measure semantic versus physical generalization abilities. This approach aims to clarify when claims of physical competence derived from VLM backbones can be meaningfully assessed.

items.title: Vision-Language Procedural Reasoning for Context-Aware Reward Modeling of Robotic Endovascular Guidewire Navigation
items.summary: Robotic endovascular guidewire navigation requires accurate control in complex patient anatomies, but current methods fail due to static reward functions lacking context awareness. This paper introduces a Vision-Language Procedural Reasoning (VL-PR) framework designed to address these limitations. The core of the system integrates a multimodal large language model (MLLM) that interprets real-time visual data to infer high-level procedural contexts. These inferred insights are then used for context-aware reward adaptation, dynamically adjusting component importance across different navigation phases. Testing on physical robotic platforms demonstrated that this approach significantly enhances task reliability and efficiency compared to static reward methods.

items.title: From Grasps to Dexterity: Large-Scale Grasp Pretraining for Dexterous Manipulation
items.summary: Existing large-scale dexterous grasp datasets primarily support grasp generation and simple pick-and-place tasks. Researchers adapted these datasets to foster functional dexterity for articulated tool use, requiring tools to be acquired, maintained, and operated. A hierarchical imitation learning framework was developed, utilizing a new 355k-trajectory dataset derived from existing grasp annotations. This low-level controller was pretrained on the grasp data and fine-tuned on specific task demonstrations within the introduced DexCraft benchmark. Evaluations showed that this method outperforms end-to-end diffusion policies in both simulation and real-world tasks, proving grasp datasets are useful for advanced contact manipulation training.

items.title: Wind and State Estimation on SE(3): Comparative Evaluation of EKF and UKF with Continuous and Discrete Quadrotor Models
items.summary: Wind velocity estimation using quadrotors is challenging due to the system's high nonlinearity. This study evaluates both continuous and discrete dynamic equations on the SE(3) manifold for this estimation task. A Lie Group Variational Integrator provides a discrete model without approximation errors. Performance was assessed using Extended Kalman Filter (EKF) and Unscented Kalman Filter (UKF) in simulations and outdoor flights. Results show that coupling discrete SE(3) dynamics with UKF achieves superior wind velocity estimation accuracy while maintaining trajectory tracking, even with low-cost sensors.

items.title: TAPE: Tether-Aware Path Planning for Autonomous Exploration of Unknown 3D Cavities Using a Tangle-Compatible Tethered Aerial Robot
items.summary: Presents TAPE, a novel method for autonomously exploring unknown three-dimensional cavities using a tethered aerial robot. The approach minimizes both traveled distance and unwound tether length by utilizing a two-level hierarchical architecture. Global planning solves a Traveling Salesman Problem (TSP) to minimize distance efficiently. Local planning further optimizes the path cost and tether length using an adjustable decision function for trade-off management. Simulations show TAPE generates only a 4.1% greater distance than pure TSP while ensuring the tether remains below its limit in 100% of simulated cases compared to 53% without it.
